title: verbascum × patrense
intro: Verbascum × hybrida, also known as Verbascum × patrense, is a hybrid of the Verbascum phoeniceum and Verbascum speciosum plants, commonly referred to as mullein. This biennial or perennial plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in its leaves, flowers, and roots, which contain mucilages, flavonoids, and saponins. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, skin conditions, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. Verbascum × patrense can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, or used topically in the form of creams, salves, and ointments.
